{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Warehouse Foreman",
  "description": "Otsuka Nigeria Nutraceutical Company (ONNC) is a fast moving FMCG, Nutraceutical, phamaceutical and chemical industry, located in sagamu, interchange, Ogun state Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Foreman

Job Location: Sagamu - Interchange, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Warehouse Foreman oversees warehouse activities including receiving, storage, inventory control, dispatch, and housekeeping.
	The role ensures materials and products are handled safely, accurately, and efficiently while supervising warehouse personnel to meet operational targets.


Key Responsibilities
Operational Supervision:


	Supervise daily warehouse operations and activities.
	Coordinate loading and unloading of goods and materials.
	Ensure proper storage, labeling, and arrangement of inventory.
	Monitor stock movement and prevent material losses or damages.
	Ensure timely issuance and dispatch of materials/products.


Inventory Control:


	Conduct routine stock checks and physical inventory counts.
	Maintain accurate warehouse records and documentation.
	Monitor inventory levels and report shortages or discrepancies.
	Support inventory reconciliation and audit processes.


Team Management:


	Supervise warehouse assistants, forklift operators, loaders, and storekeepers.
	Assign daily tasks and monitor staff performance.
	Train warehouse personnel on safety and operational procedures.
	Ensure discipline and compliance with company policies.


Safety &amp; Compliance:


	Enforce workplace safety standards and housekeeping practices.
	Ensure proper use of warehouse equipment and tools.
	Monitor compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations.
	Report accidents, damages, and unsafe conditions immediately.


Documentation &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare daily warehouse activity reports.
	Maintain records of incoming and outgoing goods.
	Coordinate with procurement, production, and logistics teams.
	Assist in preparing monthly inventory and operational reports.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	HND/B.Sc. in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or related field.
	3&ndash;5 years of warehouse experience in manufacturing, FMCG, or logistics operations.
	Supervisory experience is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of inventory management systems and warehouse procedures.
	Basic computer skills (Microsoft Excel, ERP/WMS systems).


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Inventory accuracy rate
	Timely dispatch and receiving
	Reduction in stock variance/loss
	Warehouse cleanliness and safety compliance
	Team productivity and efficiency
	Damage and wastage control


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Leadership and supervisory skills
	Inventory and stock management
	Communication and coordination skills
	Problem-solving ability
	Attention to detail
	Time management
	Safety awareness
	Ability to work under pressure


Working Conditions:


	Warehouse and industrial environment.
	May require shift work, overtime, and weekend operations.
	Frequent standing, lifting, and movement within warehouse facilities.


Salary
N200,000 - N220,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@otsuka.ng using &ldquo;Application for Warehouse Foreman&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-13",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Otsuka Nutraceutical Company Limited"
    
  },

    }

}



Warehouse Foreman Job at Otsuka Nigeria Nutraceutical Company




        
    

        
        Otsuka Nigeria Nutraceutical Company (ONNC) is a fast moving FMCG, Nutraceutical, phamaceutical and chemical industry, located in sagamu, interchange, Ogun state Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Foreman

Job Location: Sagamu - Interchange, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Warehouse Foreman oversees warehouse activities including receiving, storage, inventory control, dispatch, and housekeeping.
	The role ensures materials and products are handled safely, accurately, and efficiently while supervising warehouse personnel to meet operational targets.


Key Responsibilities
Operational Supervision:


	Supervise daily warehouse operations and activities.
	Coordinate loading and unloading of goods and materials.
	Ensure proper storage, labeling, and arrangement of inventory.
	Monitor stock movement and prevent material losses or damages.
	Ensure timely issuance and dispatch of materials/products.


Inventory Control:


	Conduct routine stock checks and physical inventory counts.
	Maintain accurate warehouse records and documentation.
	Monitor inventory levels and report shortages or discrepancies.
	Support inventory reconciliation and audit processes.


Team Management:


	Supervise warehouse assistants, forklift operators, loaders, and storekeepers.
	Assign daily tasks and monitor staff performance.
	Train warehouse personnel on safety and operational procedures.
	Ensure discipline and compliance with company policies.


Safety & Compliance:


	Enforce workplace safety standards and housekeeping practices.
	Ensure proper use of warehouse equipment and tools.
	Monitor compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations.
	Report accidents, damages, and unsafe conditions immediately.


Documentation & Reporting:


	Prepare daily warehouse activity reports.
	Maintain records of incoming and outgoing goods.
	Coordinate with procurement, production, and logistics teams.
	Assist in preparing monthly inventory and operational reports.


Qualifications & Requirements


	HND/B.Sc. in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or related field.
	3–5 years of warehouse experience in manufacturing, FMCG, or logistics operations.
	Supervisory experience is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of inventory management systems and warehouse procedures.
	Basic computer skills (Microsoft Excel, ERP/WMS systems).


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Inventory accuracy rate
	Timely dispatch and receiving
	Reduction in stock variance/loss
	Warehouse cleanliness and safety compliance
	Team productivity and efficiency
	Damage and wastage control


Skills & Competencies:


	Leadership and supervisory skills
	Inventory and stock management
	Communication and coordination skills
	Problem-solving ability
	Attention to detail
	Time management
	Safety awareness
	Ability to work under pressure


Working Conditions:


	Warehouse and industrial environment.
	May require shift work, overtime, and weekend operations.
	Frequent standing, lifting, and movement within warehouse facilities.


Salary
N200,000 - N220,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Application for Warehouse Foreman” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

Confidential Secretary Job at BDO Professional Services




        
    

        
        BDO Professional Services, a member firm of BDO International, a worldwide network of professional firms serving global and national businesses. Our global networks of resources comprise more than 1 .264 member firm offices in 144 countries. BDO international is the world’s fifth Largest multinational provider of professional services in accountancy, assurance, management consultancy, taxation and corporate advisory services. Each BDO member firm is an independent legal entity in its own country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Confidential Secretary
Job Location: Lagos

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provide executive administrative support
	Manage sensitive information with discretion
	Coordinate Internal & External communications and documentation
	Maintain records and support office operations


Requirements


	OND / HND / Bachelor’s Degree in relevant discipline
	2–5 years of experience in a similar role
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	Strong communication and organizational skills
	High attention to detail and professionalism


Key Competencies:


	Confidentiality and integrity
	Time management
	Attention to detail
	Ability to work under pressure.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed Curriculum Vitae to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Production & Procurement Assistant",
  "description": "FD Naturales is a wellness organization dedicated to providing holistic health services with the singular goal of inspiring wholeness and empowering individuals to lead healthier and better lives. Our comprehensive range of products, health programs, and services is crafted to address various facets of well-being, encompassing physical, mental, and emotional health.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Production &amp; Procurement Assistant

Job Location: Anthony, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract
Work Hours: 8:00 AM &ndash; 5:00 PM, 10AM &ndash; 2PM (Saturday)
Workdays: Monday &ndash; Saturday

Responsibilities


	Develop and implement weekly production schedules for immediate execution.
	Oversee daily production operations and adjust schedules when necessary.
	Monitor productivity, maintain product standards, and enforce quality control measures.
	Coordinate the maintenance and repair of faulty or damaged equipment.
	Liaise with suppliers, managers, clients, and other departments to ensure smooth operations.
	Collaborate with management to implement company policies and operational goals.
	Compile, analyze, and present production reports to management and the CEO.
	Conduct routine checks of products displayed in the showroom.
	Manage inventory and order necessary resources to maintain adequate stock levels.
	Ensure compliance with all health and safety regulations at all times.
	Ensure continuous availability of production materials and packaging supplies.
	Confirm that all products are properly sealed before display or distribution.
	Submit weekly activity and production reports to the Managing Director.
	Must be familiar with the use of microsoft excel
	Applicants must reside in the Anthony area or environs.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application stating why they are qualified for the role in not more than 200 words to: fdnaturales1@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  },

Production & Procurement Assistant Job at FD Naturales




        
    

        
        FD Naturales is a wellness organization dedicated to providing holistic health services with the singular goal of inspiring wholeness and empowering individuals to lead healthier and better lives. Our comprehensive range of products, health programs, and services is crafted to address various facets of well-being, encompassing physical, mental, and emotional health.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Production & Procurement Assistant

Job Location: Anthony, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract
Work Hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, 10AM – 2PM (Saturday)
Workdays: Monday – Saturday

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and implement weekly production schedules for immediate execution.
	Oversee daily production operations and adjust schedules when necessary.
	Monitor productivity, maintain product standards, and enforce quality control measures.
	Coordinate the maintenance and repair of faulty or damaged equipment.
	Liaise with suppliers, managers, clients, and other departments to ensure smooth operations.
	Collaborate with management to implement company policies and operational goals.
	Compile, analyze, and present production reports to management and the CEO.
	Conduct routine checks of products displayed in the showroom.
	Manage inventory and order necessary resources to maintain adequate stock levels.
	Ensure compliance with all health and safety regulations at all times.
	Ensure continuous availability of production materials and packaging supplies.
	Confirm that all products are properly sealed before display or distribution.
	Submit weekly activity and production reports to the Managing Director.
	Must be familiar with the use of microsoft excel
	Applicants must reside in the Anthony area or environs.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application stating why they are qualified for the role in not more than 200 words to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Information System Auditor",
  "description": "Tatum Bank is a forward-thinking, customer-centric financial institution dedicated to offering innovative banking solutions. Tatum Bank provides a full range of financial services and is focused on empowering customers with seamless, accessible banking experiences that foster financial growth, security.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Information System Auditor

Job Location: Lagos

Description


	Are you passionate about safeguarding digital infrastructure and strengthening cybersecurity systems? We&#39;re looking for a detail-oriented Information System Auditor to assess, analyze, and fortify our bank&#39;s information security practices. If you thrive on identifying vulnerabilities, conducting security audits, and driving compliance, we&#39;d love to hear from you.


What you&#39;ll be doing


	Assist in conducting penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and security audits on banking systems and networks
	Collaborate with the incident response team to investigate and respond to security breaches and protocol violations
	Assess and analyze the bank&#39;s information security systems, policies, and practices
	Execute IT audits and prepare detailed audit reports with findings and recommendations
	Monitor and ensure compliance with IT security principles, standards, and regulatory requirements
	Identify process gaps and provide recommendations for remedial actions and improvements
	Support the implementation of IT general controls across banking applications
	Conduct financial monitoring and technology risk assessments
	Stay current on emerging cybersecurity threats, trends, and best practices.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree (BSc) or its equivalent in Information Security, Computer Science, Information Technology, Electrical &amp; Electronics Engineering, or related field
	3-6 years of cognate experience within the banking or financial services industry
	Professional certification is an advantage: CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor), CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional), CISM (Certified Information Security Manager), or CIBN
	Proficiency in vulnerability management, audit execution, and IT security principles
	Strong problem-solving, analytical, and decision-making skills
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and report-writing abilities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@tatumbank.com using &quot;Application Information System Auditor&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Information System Auditor Job at Tatum Bank




        
    

        
        Tatum Bank is a forward-thinking, customer-centric financial institution dedicated to offering innovative banking solutions. Tatum Bank provides a full range of financial services and is focused on empowering customers with seamless, accessible banking experiences that foster financial growth, security.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Information System Auditor

Job Location: Lagos

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Are you passionate about safeguarding digital infrastructure and strengthening cybersecurity systems? We're looking for a detail-oriented Information System Auditor to assess, analyze, and fortify our bank's information security practices. If you thrive on identifying vulnerabilities, conducting security audits, and driving compliance, we'd love to hear from you.


What you'll be doing


	Assist in conducting penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and security audits on banking systems and networks
	Collaborate with the incident response team to investigate and respond to security breaches and protocol violations
	Assess and analyze the bank's information security systems, policies, and practices
	Execute IT audits and prepare detailed audit reports with findings and recommendations
	Monitor and ensure compliance with IT security principles, standards, and regulatory requirements
	Identify process gaps and provide recommendations for remedial actions and improvements
	Support the implementation of IT general controls across banking applications
	Conduct financial monitoring and technology risk assessments
	Stay current on emerging cybersecurity threats, trends, and best practices.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree (BSc) or its equivalent in Information Security, Computer Science, Information Technology, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, or related field
	3-6 years of cognate experience within the banking or financial services industry
	Professional certification is an advantage: CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor), CISSP (Certified Information Systems Security Professional), CISM (Certified Information Security Manager), or CIBN
	Proficiency in vulnerability management, audit execution, and IT security principles
	Strong problem-solving, analytical, and decision-making skills
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and report-writing abilities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Application Information System Auditor" as the subject of the email.
